claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children would you like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 15 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great that Alex is 15 years old, as Child Benefit typically covers children under 16.

Now, I have a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

**Are you responsible for Alex? This usually means either:**
- You live with Alex, or
- You're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them – for example on food, clothes, or pocket money

Which of these applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely, thank you.

Just a couple more questions to make sure we have a complete picture:

**Is Alex currently in any of the following situations?**
- In hospital or residential care
- In local authority care
- Working 24 hours or more per week
- Receiving certain benefits in their own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply. Alex isn't in hospital or care, doesn't work that much, and isn't receiving any benefits.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

One final question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm pleased to tell you that **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

**For Alex (age 15):** ✅ Eligible
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You live in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex as they live with you
- Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation
- Alex is not working 24+ hours per week or receiving benefits
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

**What to do next:**
You can make a claim for Child Benefit through the official government channels. You'll need to provide details about yourself and Alex when applying.

Just so you're aware, when Alex turns 16, you may still be able to continue receiving Child Benefit if they stay in approved education or training until they turn 20.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 15 years old, which is under the age of 16. The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for Alex as Alex lives with them. Alex is not in hospital, residential care, or local authority care. Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week and is not receiving benefits in their own right. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for one child, Alex (aged 15), who lives with them. Alex is under 16, not in care or hospital, not working significant hours, and not receiving benefits. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
